modules.description: Demo exposure analysis for exported identities and tokens.

findings.recommendation: Move the export into controlled storage and remove plaintext copies from the workstation.
findings.summary: A local campaign planning export contains a plain business contact list.
findings.title: Plaintext email address collection detected

findings.recommendation: Rotate the affected session or API token and remove plaintext notes exports.
findings.summary: A notes export contains a reusable session artifact that should not live in plaintext storage.
findings.title: Session token artifact detected in analyst notes

findings.recommendation: Rotate related credentials and add MFA to the identity set used in public-facing services.
findings.summary: finance.ops@example.com appears in one offline breach archive used for the demo scenario.
findings.title: Identity exposure detected in breach intelligence

findings.recommendation: Replace the reused secret with a unique passphrase generated for this account set.
findings.summary: A staged credential candidate matches a common-password blocklist entry.
findings.title: Common password detected

findings.recommendation: Assign unique passwords to each service account and enforce MFA where supported.
findings.summary: Two demo service accounts share the same password candidate.
findings.title: Password reuse detected

findings.recommendation: Delete the export and move any required secrets into a managed vault.
findings.summary: A local browser export bundle includes remembered account details in an unprotected folder.
findings.title: Credential store artifact detected

findings.recommendation: Disable local password storage and move credentials into a managed password manager.
findings.summary: The primary Chromium profile stores saved logins for multiple business services.
findings.title: Saved logins present in browser profile

findings.recommendation: Enable password leak detection and monitor for reused credentials.
findings.summary: Password leak detection is disabled in the main browser profile.
findings.title: Leak detection protection disabled

findings.recommendation: Do not open the message, block the sender, and brief the affected user group.
findings.summary: A vendor-themed email references a deceptive login URL with account pressure wording.
findings.title: Suspicious URLs detected in email body

findings.recommendation: Review the destination domain before allowing it through mail or web controls.
findings.summary: The domain in the staged email appears in a suspicious-domain feed used for the demo.
findings.title: Threat intelligence match for domain

recommended_actions: Replace reused credentials and enable MFA on exposed services.
Remove plaintext token notes and browser password exports from local storage.
Block the staged phishing domain and notify impacted users.
metadata.risk_explanation: The exposure set is still containable, but password reuse and browser-stored credentials lower the effort required for account takeover.
metadata.summary: DIPS demo mode shows early identity leakage, weak credential hygiene, and emerging phishing pressure with limited but credible exposure.

suspicious_patterns.summary: A reused credential appears alongside a breach hit, increasing the chance of credential stuffing.
suspicious_patterns.title: Password reuse overlaps with early breach exposure

notes: Demo mode generated synthetic findings. No live scan was executed.
All domains, identities, and tokens in this report are staged examples.

contributing_findings: identity_exposure: Plaintext email address collection detected
identity_exposure: Session token artifact detected in analyst notes
breach_intelligence: Identity exposure detected in breach intelligence
top_recommendations: Move the export into controlled storage and remove plaintext copies from the workstation.
Assign unique passwords to each service account and enforce MFA where supported.
Replace the reused secret with a unique passphrase generated for this account set.
Rotate the affected session or API token and remove plaintext notes exports.
Rotate related credentials and add MFA to the identity set used in public-facing services.
Delete the export and move any required secrets into a managed vault.

correlations: Password reuse overlaps with early breach exposure
events.summary: Two service accounts share the same secret.
events.title: Password reuse detected

correlations: Password reuse overlaps with early breach exposure
events.summary: An operational mailbox appears in one breach archive.
events.title: Identity exposure detected in breach intelligence

events.summary: The staged vendor email contains a deceptive link.
events.title: Suspicious URLs detected in email body

events.summary: The linked domain is already tagged as suspicious.
events.title: Threat intelligence match for domain

patterns.name: Password reuse overlaps with early breach exposure
patterns.summary: Credential hygiene and breach history combine into a credible account-takeover path.
